name: Ghost of Conyberry
author: Unknown
description:
Ghost of Conyberry — Legends collected from tavern verse, elder memory, and sources no scholar will swear to in court. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1372 DR.

Each generation adds a verse; purists claim the oldest layer is true and everything since is flattery.

name: Gorgomorz - Daggerford's Devil
author: Hilgraff the Scribe
description:
Gorgomorz - Daggerford's Devil — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Hilgraff the Scribe is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1270–1372 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Grand History of the Realms
author: Elminster
description:
Grand History of the Realms — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Elminster is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 15th century DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Gloryswords: An Informal and Incomplete Overview of Adventuring Bands in the Year of the Spur
author: Ragefast
description:
Gloryswords: An Informal and Incomplete Overview of Adventuring Bands in the Year of the Spur — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Ragefast is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1369 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Gaurdront's Guide to Monstrous Beasts
author: Gaurdront
description:
Gaurdront's Guide to Monstrous Beasts — Natural history from hunters, riverfolk, and sages who went outside and wrote down what bit them. Gaurdront is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to before 1372 DR.

Seasonal addenda in surviving copies suggest the author revised the work every year they lived to see spring.
